hook: You already know how to work with numbers and variables — now imagine grouping expressions into powerful building blocks called polynomials. The missing piece is learning how to build them up through multiplication and then reverse-engineer them through factoring. What if breaking apart an expression were the key to solving equations that once seemed impossible?
concept: This unit is about understanding polynomials as structures you can assemble and disassemble — building them up through addition and multiplication, then pulling them apart through factoring to reveal hidden solutions, a process formally known as polynomial arithmetic and factorization.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to combine polynomials by identifying and collecting like terms to simplify expressions cleanly and accurately.
Master distributing every term across every other term to expand products of polynomials using the distributive property.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Special Products (Difference of Squares, Perfect Squares)
topic_roadmap.description: Apply shortcut patterns like $(a+b)^2 = a^2 + 2ab + b^2$ and $(a+b)(a-b) = a^2 - b^2$ to multiply special polynomial pairs quickly and confidently.
You will learn to reverse distribution by pulling out the greatest common factor from a polynomial to write it as a simpler product.
Factor trinomials of the form $x^2 + bx + c$ by finding two numbers that multiply to $c$ and add to $b$.
Apply advanced factoring strategies, such as the $ac$-method, to break apart trinomials $ax^2 + bx + c$ when the leading coefficient is not 1.
Recognize and factor special polynomial forms — including difference of squares and perfect square trinomials — by matching them to known patterns.
You will solve polynomial equations by factoring completely and applying the Zero Product Property to find all solutions.
